db:abstract.db:para: Invokes a dynamically loaded nested state machine.

db:article.db:para: Invoke Example (Dynamic) demonstrates how to use the <invoke> element with generated nested state-machines, where the SCXML file is dynamically loaded. The <invoke> element is used to create an instance of an external service.

db:section.db:title: Running the Example
db:section.db:para: To run the example from Qt Creator, open the Welcome mode and select the example from Examples. For more information, visit Building and Running an Example.

db:section.db:title: Invoking the State Machine
db:section.db:para: In statemachine.scxml, we specify a state machine with the name Directions of type http://www.w3.org/TR/scxml/ to invoke:

db:section.db:title: Dynamically Loading the State Machine
db:section.db:para: We link against the Qt SCXML module by adding the following line to the project build files.
db:section.db:programlisting:
find_package(Qt6 REQUIRED COMPONENTS Core Gui Qml Scxml)
target_link_libraries(invoke-dynamic PUBLIC Qt6::Core Qt6::Gui Qt6::Qml Qt6::Scxml )
db:section.db:para: We dynamically create the state machine, as follows:
